subject: Human Resources
title: The Four-Day Workweek
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Four-Day Workweek.

hook.text: Iceland tested a four-day workweek with 2,500 workers. Productivity stayed the same or improved. Burnout dropped.

buildup.text: The four-day workweek reduces hours without reducing pay—typically 32 hours across four days instead of 40 across five.

discovery.text: The UK's 2022 pilot with 61 companies found 92% kept the four-day week—citing better retention, less burnout, and equal output.

twist.text: Not all industries adapt easily—healthcare, manufacturing, and service roles require coverage models complicating shorter weeks.

climax.text: The four-day week forces efficiency—cutting unnecessary meetings, streamlining processes, and focusing on output over hours.

punchline.text: We assumed 40 hours was optimal. Nobody ever tested it. Now they have.

quiz.question: What percentage of UK pilot companies continued the four-day week?
